blocks.text: DTC P0630: Frame number not written

The PGM-FI ECU stores frame number information in EEPROM and outputs frame number information in response to a request command from a scan tool (including HDS). The frame number is registered to the PGM-FI ECU for each vehicle using a scan tool (including HDS) when the PGM-FI ECU is exchanged on the vehicle production line or in service. The registered frame number is read from the EEPROM by the CPU after the ignition is turned on or a clear command is executed. If the frame number is not registered in the EEPROM when the ignition is turned on or the clear command is executed, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the frame number is not registered and stores the DTC.

blocks.text: When the frame number is not registered in the EEPROM.

items.text: Frame number not registered in EEPROM in PGM-FI ECU

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Upon completion of frame number registration, the PGM-FI warning light turns off.
